metadata.maturity_notes: Lite diagnostic variant; inherits the parent maturity tier but should be evaluated separately because it has smaller scope and less bespoke hook coverage.
metadata.challenge_summary: Individual treasure temptation can damage team outcome, forcing trust, reporting, and reward alignment.
dilemmas: Keeping gold improves local score but worsens final pressure
Honest accounting may lower treasure reward
One hidden pickup can hurt both heroes
warden_runbook: Punish concealed loot only after fair warning
metadata.lite_focus: A three-room accounting test: one chest, one objective, one optional stash, and explicit need to communicate inventory.
non_copy_safety_notes: Original compact layout and original scenario text.
Targets abstract cooperative dungeon-crawl dynamics rather than protected quest content.
Uses MARL challenge metadata as the design contract.
metadata.classic_dynamic_role_dilemma: balance optional treasure against Warden dread and extraction tempo
metadata.classic_dynamic_warden_pressure: convert greedy treasure searches into dread and wandering pressure
metadata.classic_dynamic_extraction_hook: greedy heroes can extract partially but lower team quality

entry_room.name: Entry Room
entry_room.description: The entry into Magistrate Gold is intentionally small: enough space to form a plan, not enough to hide poor coordination.

objective_room.name: Objective Room
objective_room.description: The core object of the lite challenge is visible here; taking it should trigger extraction thinking, not exhaustive exploration.

side_cache.name: Side Cache
side_cache.description: An optional route/cache tests whether the agents understand the dungeon's MARL axis instead of chasing reward blindly.

furniture.name: Counting Slate
furniture.description: Primary clue object for mixed-motive sequential social dilemma. It should teach agents what kind of coordination this dungeon rewards.

furniture.name: Optional Cache
furniture.description: Optional reward that is safe only when the team has solved the core coordination problem first.

achievements.title: Read the Lite Clue
achievements.description: Search the focus object that explains mixed-motive sequential social dilemma.

achievements.title: Open the First Lite Door
achievements.description: Open the first door and trigger a reveal-boundary planning step.

achievements.title: Send a Coordination Message
achievements.description: Use communication instead of silent independent wandering.

scripts.lite_design_note: A three-room accounting test: one chest, one objective, one optional stash, and explicit need to communicate inventory.

core_failure_modes: failure to protect the coordination-critical agent

preferred_tactics: Punish concealed loot only after fair warning
convert greedy treasure searches into dread and wandering pressure
forbidden_tactics: spawn pressure without a visible or scripted cue
use unrevealed map geometry for perfect counterplay
erase the dungeon-specific lesson with generic monster spam

classic_dynamic.warden: convert greedy treasure searches into dread and wandering pressure
classic_dynamic.extraction: greedy heroes can extract partially but lower team quality

monster_activation.notes: Lite variants keep monsters dormant until reveal so smaller LLMs can replan at clear boundaries.

search_categories.notes: Lite variants emphasize explicit search/action choices and communication.
